message:
fix(replay): fail closed on zero-baseline telemetry; simplify session picker WAL test

extension_replay.rs:
- Reorder compute_overhead_per_mille to check baseline_micros == 0
  before captured <= baseline, so zero-baseline observations correctly
  return u32::MAX (invalid) instead of 0 (no overhead). This prevents
  replay capture from being silently allowed when baseline telemetry
  was never collected.
- Add capture_gate_fails_closed_when_zero_baseline_reports_zero_capture_cost
  regression test confirming DisabledByInvalidBaseline verdict

session_picker.rs:
- Simplify build_meta_from_sqlite_uses_session_file_stats test to use
  the shared session_file_stats() helper instead of manually creating
  WAL/SHM files and computing expected values, reducing test complexity
  and coupling

Also update .beads/issues.jsonl with new tracking issues bd-1o1n0 and
bd-1vmef.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1nzu7","title":"Session index upsert should clear stale session names","description":"SessionIndex::upsert_meta currently writes name=COALESCE(excluded.name, sessions.name). If a later persisted snapshot has no session name, the SQLite index keeps the prior stale name instead of reflecting the snapshot. This can leave resume/session-picker metadata inconsistent with the actual session file. Fix the upsert to store the incoming nullable name verbatim and add a focused regression.","notes":"Patched src/session_index.rs so SessionIndex::upsert_meta stores name=excluded.name and added index_session_update_clears_stale_session_name regression. Validation completed: cargo fmt --check passed; env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/pi_agent_rust_orangelantern TMPDIR=/tmp cargo test --lib index_session_update_clears_stale_session_name -- --nocapture passed locally after cold build; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/pi_agent_rust_orangelantern_check TMPDIR=/tmp cargo check --all-targets passed on worker vmi1149989. Earlier cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings failures were on unrelated shared-tree files before PearlDune’s permissions/extensions fixes, not this session_index patch. UBS file-scoped scan on src/session_index.rs surfaced only generic test/heuristic noise, no concrete new defect on the patch.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-08T23:19:52.686887501Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-08T23:46:09.065744823Z","closed_at":"2026-03-08T23:46:09.065719165Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-1o1n0","title":"Recover poisoned VCR env override mutex in env_var","description":"Fresh-eyes review of recent VCR helper changes found that src/vcr.rs::env_var() ignores poisoned TEST_ENV_OVERRIDES locks and falls through to the real process environment.  {"id":"bd-1o4","title":"E2E CLI scenarios: tool enable/disable + error paths","description":"Goal:
- Add E2E tests that validate tool enable/disable behavior and common error paths with detailed logging.

Scope:
- `--tools` selection impacts available tool list in system prompt.
- `--no-tools` disables all tools and confirms tool-call failures are surfaced.
- Error paths: invalid model/provider, missing API key, rpc mode restrictions.
- Validate that error messages are clear and actionable.

Logging Requirements:
- Use CLI E2E harness (bd-1wc) + TestLogger (bd-3ml).
- Log effective tool list, system prompt excerpt, VCR_MODE, and cassette name/path.
- Capture stdout/stderr and assertion diffs on failure.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Tests capture stdout/stderr and confirm error messages.
- Logs show tool registry inputs + effective tool list + prompt excerpts + cassette id.
- No network access; VCR playback used where streaming needed.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-1o6l","title":"Extension load-time benchmarks (all 60 official, TS vs Rust)","description":"# Extension load-time benchmarks (all 60 official, TS vs Rust)

## What
For every official extension, measure load time in both runtimes. The differential runner already captures load_time_ms -- this task aggregates and analyzes the results.

## Analysis
- P50, P95, P99 load times for each runtime
- Per-extension comparison table
- Identify outliers (extensions much slower in Rust)
- Root cause for slow Rust loads (swc compilation? QuickJS init? module resolution?)

## Target
- P99 < 100ms for Rust
- Rust <= 2x TypeScript for any extension
- Rust median <= TypeScript median

## Output
- Benchmark report: tests/ext_conformance/reports/load_time_benchmark.json
- Visualization-ready data","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:25:17.634261428Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T18:28:29.920701184Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T18:28:29.920635040Z","close_reason":"Added TS harness + Rust timed load capture in tests/ext_conformance_diff.rs; generated tests/ext_conformance/reports/load_time_benchmark.json via load_time_benchmark_official","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1o6l","depends_on_id":"bd-139x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1o6l","depends_on_id":"bd-150s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}

Specify the schema for a large extension corpus manifest that remains deterministic and machine-checkable.

Required fields
- source (repo, commit/tag, path)
- entrypoint + runtime tier
- dependency style + capabilities
- checksum/provenance
- scenario suite linkage

Output
A schema (JSON) plus validation rules to prevent drift.

 {"id":"bd-1ve4","title":"CI: add cross-platform build/test matrix (linux/macOS/windows)","description":"# Goal
Increase release confidence by running CI on all target platforms (at minimum build + unit tests), and document any platform-specific exclusions.

# Background
The current CI workflow only runs on `ubuntu-latest`. That’s good for fast iteration but insufficient for shipping binaries.

# Scope
- Add a GitHub Actions matrix over:
  - `ubuntu-latest`
  - `macos-latest`
  - `windows-latest`
- For each OS:
  - `cargo fmt --check`
  - `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
  - `cargo test --all-targets`

# Platform Considerations
- System deps:
  - `fd` and `rg` are required/optional for tool tests; install equivalents per OS.
- Path dependencies:
  - CI currently clones sibling repos (asupersync/rich_rust/charmed_rust/sqlmodel_rust). Ensure the same approach works on macOS/Windows.
- VCR playback mode:
  - Keep `VCR_MODE=playback` for determinism.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `ci.yml` runs on Linux/macOS/Windows.
- [ ] Any OS-specific skips are explicit and justified (no silent test omissions).
- [ ] Failures include enough logs to debug platform-specific issues.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-1vfi","title":"E2E infra: discover runnable providers from models/auth/env at runtime","description":"Implement provider/key discovery used by live E2E tests so the suite automatically tests every provider that is actually configured.

Scope:
- Build deterministic discovery logic that merges:
  1) ~/.pi/agent/models.json (or configured model registry path)
  2) auth store entries (api_key/oauth)
  3) environment variables
- Produce a normalized provider matrix (provider id, model id, auth source, required config fields, enabled/disabled reason).
- Ensure discovery supports OpenAI-compatible providers (OpenRouter, xAI, DeepSeek, etc.) in addition to first-party providers.
- Emit discovery report artifact (JSON + markdown summary) before test execution.

Requirements:
- Redact secrets in logs/artifacts while preserving enough context for debugging.
- If models.json path is missing or malformed, tests should fail with actionable diagnostics (not silent skip).
- Discovery output must be the single source of truth for provider test selection.

 {"id":"bd-1vo","title":"Migrate OpenAI provider to asupersync HTTP","description":"# Migrate OpenAI provider to asupersync HTTP

## Goal
Replace reqwest usage in src/providers/openai.rs with asupersync HTTP client.

## Background
OpenAI provider handles:
- Chat completions API
- Function calling (tools)
- SSE streaming with data: [DONE] termination
- 645 lines, 3 unit tests

## Implementation
Same pattern as Anthropic migration:
1. Replace reqwest::Client with crate::http::Client
2. Replace request building with asupersync RequestBuilder
3. Replace SSE streaming with SseStream adapter
4. Add Cx parameter to async functions

## OpenAI-Specific Considerations
1. **SSE Format**: Uses "data: [DONE]" as termination marker
2. **Tool Calls**: JSON in function_call field
3. **Streaming Deltas**: content delta in choices[0].delta.content
4. **Error Format**: Different error JSON structure from Anthropic

## Dependencies
- bd-9sa (HTTP client wrapper)
- bd-pwz (SSE streaming adapter)
- bd-37l (Anthropic migration first - establishes pattern)

## Testing
- 3 existing unit tests must pass
- Integration test with mock server

## Files
- src/providers/openai.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No reqwest imports
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Streaming works
- [ ] Tool calls work","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)

[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented

 {"id":"bd-3avm","title":"Task: Implement session events (session_before_switch, session_before_fork)","description":"# Task: Implement Session Events (Cancellable)

## Objective

Dispatch session_before_switch and session_before_fork events, allowing extensions to cancel the operation.

## Events Covered

### session_before_switch Event
- **When**: Before switching to a different session
- **Purpose**: Allow extensions to block session switch
- **Can Cancel**: Yes (return false)

### session_before_fork Event
- **When**: Before forking a new session
- **Purpose**: Allow extensions to block fork
- **Can Cancel**: Yes (return false)

## Cancellation Semantics

Unlike other events, session events are cancellable:
- Handler returns `false` → operation cancelled
- Handler returns `true` or nothing → operation proceeds
- Handler throws → log error, proceed (fail-open)

## Implementation

```rust
impl SessionManager {
    pub async fn switch_session(
        &mut self,
        target_path: &Path,
        event_dispatcher: Option<&EventDispatcher>,
    ) -> Result<SwitchResult> {
        // Dispatch session_before_switch
        if let Some(dispatcher) = event_dispatcher {
            if dispatcher.has_handlers("session_before_switch") {
                let event = ExtensionEvent::SessionBeforeSwitch {
                    current_session: self.current_file().map(|p| p.to_string_lossy().to_string()),
                    target_session: target_path.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
                };
                
                // Check if any handler cancels
                let result = dispatcher.dispatch::<bool>(event).await?;
                if result == Some(false) {
                    return Ok(SwitchResult::Cancelled);
                }
            }
        }
        
        // Proceed with switch
        self.load_session(target_path).await?;
        Ok(SwitchResult::Switched)
    }
    
    pub async fn fork_session(
        &mut self,
        entry_id: &str,
        event_dispatcher: Option<&EventDispatcher>,
    ) -> Result<ForkResult> {
        // Dispatch session_before_fork
        if let Some(dispatcher) = event_dispatcher {
            if dispatcher.has_handlers("session_before_fork") {
                let event = ExtensionEvent::SessionBeforeFork {
                    current_session: self.current_file().map(|p| p.to_string_lossy().to_string()),
                    fork_entry_id: entry_id.to_string(),
                };
                
                let result = dispatcher.dispatch::<bool>(event).await?;
                if result == Some(false) {
                    return Ok(ForkResult::Cancelled);
                }
            }
        }
        
        // Proceed with fork
        let new_session = self.create_fork(entry_id).await?;
        Ok(ForkResult::Forked(new_session))
    }
}

#[derive(Debug)]
pub enum SwitchResult {
    Switched,
    Cancelled,
}

#[derive(Debug)]
pub enum ForkResult {
    Forked(SessionPath),
    Cancelled,
}
```

## Handler Example (Extension)

```javascript
// Prevent switching away from unsaved work
ctx.on("session_before_switch", async (event) => {
    const hasUnsavedWork = await checkUnsavedWork(event.currentSession);
    if (hasUnsavedWork) {
        ctx.ui.notify({ 
            title: "Unsaved Work",
            message: "Cannot switch sessions with unsaved changes",
            level: "warning"
        });
        return false; // Cancel switch
    }
    return true; // Allow switch
});
```

## RPC Protocol

In RPC mode, the response includes `cancelled: boolean`:

```json
// Command
{"type": "switch_session", "sessionPath": "/path/to/session.jsonl"}

// Response (if cancelled)
{"type": "response", "command": "switch_session", "success": true, "data": {"cancelled": true}}
```

## Testing

1. Unit test: No handler → proceeds
2. Unit test: Handler returns true → proceeds
3. Unit test: Handler returns false → cancelled
4. Unit test: Handler throws → proceeds (fail-open)
5. Integration test: Extension blocks session switch
6. Integration test: RPC returns cancelled flag

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-tg4w (EventDispatcher)
- Related: Session management (src/session.rs)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] session_before_switch dispatched
- [ ] session_before_fork dispatched
- [ ] Returning false cancels operation
- [ ] Errors don't block operation
- [ ] RPC protocol compatibility
- [ ] Unit tests pass","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:58:14.884156499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T00:01:18.427287720Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T00:01:18.427215986Z","close_reason":"Implemented cancellable session_before_switch for /new and /resume; added tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3avm","depends_on_id":"bd-10vp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3avm","depends_on_id":"bd-tg4w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ay7","title":"Generate validated extension manifest with capability classification","description":"# Generate validated extension manifest with capability classification

## Context
After static scan and dynamic validation, we need a SINGLE authoritative manifest that says:
- Which extensions are valid (loadable in pi-mono)
- What capabilities each uses (tools, events, commands, UI, providers, etc.)
- What tier each belongs to (simple, medium, complex, multi-file, UI-heavy, etc.)
- What mocks each needs (HTTP, exec, FS, session, UI)

This manifest drives ALL subsequent conformance testing phases.

## Manifest Schema (proposed)
{
  "schema": "pi.ext.validated-manifest.v1",
  "generated_at": "...",
  "pi_mono_version": "...",
  "extensions": [
    {
      "id": "hello",
      "entry_path": "tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/hello/hello.ts",
      "source_tier": "official-pi-mono",
      "ts_load_status": "ok",
      "ts_load_time_ms": 42,
      "capabilities": {
        "registers_tools": false,
        "registers_commands": true,
        "registers_flags": false,
        "registers_shortcuts": false,
        "registers_providers": false,
        "registers_message_renderers": false,
        "subscribes_events": ["tool_call"],
        "uses_exec": false,
        "uses_http": false,
        "uses_ui": true,
        "uses_session": false,
        "is_multi_file": false,
        "has_npm_deps": false
      },
      "conformance_tier": 1,
      "mock_requirements": ["ui"],
      "registrations": {
        "tools": [],
        "commands": [{"name": "/hello", "...": "..."}],
        "flags": [],
        "shortcuts": [],
        "event_handlers": ["tool_call"]
      }
    }
  ]
}

## Tier Classification Rules
- Tier 1 (Simple): Single event handler or single command, no external deps
- Tier 2 (Medium): Multiple capabilities, no external deps
- Tier 3 (Complex): Multi-file, external deps, or provider registration
- Tier 4 (UI-Heavy): Overlay, widget, editor, or game extensions
- Tier 5 (Platform-Specific): Mac-only, requires specific tools, etc.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every validated extension has a manifest entry
- Capabilities accurately reflect what the extension registers
- Tier classification is correct (verified by spot-checking 10+ extensions)
- Manifest is committed at tests/ext_conformance/VALIDATED_MANIFEST.json","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:18:15.935485181Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:58:46.742160995Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T07:58:46.742067471Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ay7","depends_on_id":"bd-2dnl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:12Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3bje","title":"Build extension conformance test harness (scenario runner + diff engine)","description":"Create a Rust test harness that can:
1. Load any extension into the QuickJS runtime
2. Execute predefined scenarios (tool calls, event dispatches, command invocations)
3. Capture all outputs (tool results, UI calls, hostcall sequences, state mutations)
4. Diff captured outputs against reference fixtures (from TypeScript capture)
5. Produce structured JSONL test reports with pass/fail/diff details

- VCR playback for HTTP/exec mocks
- Configurable UI stubs (simulating user select/confirm responses)
- Timeout enforcement per extension (5s default)
- Parallel test execution (extensions must be isolated)
- Both single-extension and batch modes

 {"id":"bd-3br2a","title":"[SEC-2.2] Deterministic extension lockfile and provenance verification pipeline","description":"## Background
Reproducible installs and integrity checks are required to prevent dependency drift and tampering.

## Scope
- Define lockfile format capturing source, version, digest, and trust state.
- Verify package digests/signatures during install/update.
- Fail closed on mismatch; emit explicit remediation instructions.

## Deliverables
- Lockfile format doc and validation implementation.
- Verification diagnostics with reason codes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Same inputs produce identical lockfile artifacts.
- [ ] Digest/provenance mismatch blocks install/update by default.
- [ ] Trust state transitions are recorded in audit artifacts.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description is implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests added/updated for success, failure, edge cases, and determinism where applicable
[ ] E2E scripts added/updated for benign flow, adversarial flow, and rollback/recovery flow relevant to this bead
[ ] E2E runs emit structured JSONL logs with: timestamp, issue_id, extension_id, capability, policy_profile, score, reason_codes, action, latency_ms, correlation_id, and redaction_summary
[ ] Artifact manifest is deterministic and linked in bead comments (paths + checksums/identifiers)
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test (plus targeted conformance/security suites)
[ ] If runtime behavior changed, docs/config examples are updated; if no behavior changed, explicitly record N/A rationale for e2e changes","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:39:39.307157940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T08:27:12.538226120Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T08:27:12.538134320Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["provenance","security","supply-chain"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3br2a","depends_on_id":"bd-f0huc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:56Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":2197,"issue_id":"bd-3br2a","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Opus agent claiming bd-3br2a. Will implement: (1) lockfile format with source, version, digest, trust state; (2) provenance verification during install/update; (3) fail-closed on mismatch with remediation diagnostics.","created_at":"2026-02-14T08:10:26Z"},{"id":2198,"issue_id":"bd-3br2a","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed SEC-2.2. Delivered:
1. docs/security/lockfile-format.md — full specification of pi.package_lock.v1 schema, digest computation, verification semantics, trust state transitions, fail-closed guarantees, audit log format
2. Made lockfile types public (PackageLockfile, PackageLockEntry, PackageSourceKind, PackageResolvedProvenance, PackageEntryTrustState, PackageTrustAuditEvent, LockTransitionPlan, PackageLockMismatch, PackageLockAction) for cross-module validation
3. Made key functions public (evaluate_lock_transition, read_package_lockfile, write_package_lockfile_atomic, digest_package_path, sort_lock_entries)
4. Created tests/extension_lockfile_provenance.rs with 28 integration tests covering: schema stability, JSON roundtrip, first-seen trust, digest mismatch fail-closed, provenance mismatch fail-closed, unpinned update allows changes, pinned rejects changes, deterministic digest computation, CR normalization, .git exclusion, entry sorting, lockfile read/write roundtrip, byte determinism, trust state serde, source kind serde, mismatch diagnostics, sequential install→verify→update→tamper cycle
5. All 128 integration tests + 122 lib tests pass","created_at":"2026-02-14T08:26:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3bs","title":"Tooling: Compatibility scanner + evidence ledger","description":"# Goal
Ship a scanner that flags Node/Bun-only APIs and produces an **evidence ledger** for compatibility decisions.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Static scan for unsupported imports/APIs, including:
  - `node:*` builtins
  - bare builtin specifiers (`fs`, `path`, `child_process`, ...)
  - common risky constructs (e.g. `new Function`, `eval`) as *flagged* (unless explicitly forbidden)
- Output: list of required capabilities and reasons (line-level evidence).
- Actionable remediation hints.
- Stable, deterministic output ordering for diffability.

# Alien-Artifact Angle
- Evidence ledger is human-auditable and deterministic.
- Every rejection points to exact source locations.

# Tests
- Unit fixtures for scan results and ordering.
- Fixture proving the pinned sample set yields a stable ledger.
- E2E harness validates ledger presence in logs.

# Dependencies
- Depends on the rewrite contract (bd-2ki) so the scanner’s findings match what extc can actually rewrite.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
